topic: EXP-289 data-source parenthetical updated to geomagnetic-domain sources
change_summary: EXP-289 substring replace in detail_verdict_text: '(IGRF, NASA station archives, ocean-tide gauges, telluric records, etc.)' → 'IGRF-14 / NOAA WMM2025 — derived from the INTERMAGNET observatory network'. Wrapper sentence unchanged. ISS-1863/ISS-1864 cleanup.
our_argument_summary: WIN-060 verdict 'Std Model Explains' rests on the dome having no core-flow mechanism to predict SAA western-cell drift rate; standard MHD (Finlay 2016) derives the rate quantitatively from CMB flow inversions while the dome merely extrapolates the same drift data. Detail_verdict_text adds the post-hoc-test wrapper noting no monitor.py entry exists. EXP-289 patches the data-source parenthetical from generic to geomagnetic-specific.

relevant_files: raw-text/02-wins.txt — SAA western-cell drift dome registration
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: EXP-289 is a string_replace_first_occurrence operation on detail_verdict_text. It does not touch detail_evidence, detail_claim, finding, claim, verdict, or any code_analysis tag. The wrapper sentence ('The confirming value comes from a field-specific published dataset ... that was publicly available at the time of the dome monitor commit ...') is preserved verbatim. Patch arithmetic verified by reading WIN-060.detail_verdict_text in the fresh clone — the new parenthetical is present and the wrapper is intact.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: n/a — patch is a static-substring substitution.
data_sources_used: IGRF-14 (International Geomagnetic Reference Field, 14th generation, 2024 release)
NOAA WMM2025 (World Magnetic Model 2025, Dec 2024 release)
INTERMAGNET observatory network (real-time magnetometer network, ~150 stations)

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Per detail_verdict_text 'no monitor.py entry exists for this value at all', no monitoring code path. Tag preserved (untouched by EXP-289).
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Standard MHD (Finlay 2016 CMB flow inversions) explains westward drift rate. Dome adopts NOAA/ESA drift data and relabels. Tag preserved.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Westward drift documented for centuries. Tag preserved.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No dome geometric parameter produces drift rate. Tag preserved.

holes_found.description: All three entries are unambiguously domain-appropriate for SAA / geomagnetic westward-drift studies. IGRF-14 is the current International Geomagnetic Reference Field generation. WMM2025 is NOAA's December-2024 World Magnetic Model release. INTERMAGNET is the real-time magnetometer observatory network. No imprecision flagged. Compared to the Sondrestrom imprecision in the WIN-002 batch companion, WIN-060's patch is cleaner.

WIN-060's detail_verdict_text contains a notable specific weaker test ('no monitor.py entry exists for this value at all — the dome author has not included it in the monitoring suite'). The patch doesn't touch this sentence — it remains intact. This 'no monitor.py entry' framing is rhetorically strong because the dome's own definition of timestamping requires a monitor entry to exist; without one, the weaker commit-timestamp test isn't even applicable. Worth preserving in any future edit.
holes_found.recommendation: If future edits touch WIN-060 detail_verdict_text, preserve the 'no monitor.py entry exists for this value at all' framing — it's the rhetorical anchor of the post-hoc-test argument for this WIN.

kernel_of_truth.description: Westward magnetic drift is a real, multi-century-documented phenomenon. The SAA western cell IS drifting westward, and IGRF-14/WMM2025/INTERMAGNET data confirm this directly (data/wins.json WIN-060 detail_evidence). The dome\'s engagement with this data is genuine — it\'s pointing at a real signal, not noise.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: But the verdict 'Std Model Explains' rests on the dome having no INDEPENDENT mechanism to predict drift rate or direction (data/wins.json WIN-060 finding: 'It has no core-flow mechanism'). Standard MHD via Finlay 2016 derives the rate from CMB flow inversions; the dome merely extrapolates the same NOAA/ESA data. The patch sharpens the post-hoc-test paragraph by naming the actual data sources, which underscores the point: the dome is consuming the same standard-model products it\'s claiming to predict.

deeper_analysis:
BATCHED REVIEW (2 of 2). Companion: WIN-002.

Scope of the patch: substring replacement in detail_verdict_text only. The wrapper sentence — which is the actual load-bearing part of the post-hoc-test argument — is unchanged. The structurally important sentence 'no monitor.py entry exists for this value at all — the dome author has not included it in the monitoring suite' is also untouched and remains the rhetorical anchor of the WIN-060 post-hoc-test argument.

PATCH CORRECTNESS: confirmed by reading WIN-060 detail_verdict_text in the fresh clone — the new geomagnetic-domain parenthetical is present, the surrounding wrapper is intact, and no other field was disturbed.

PER-WIN DOMAIN CHECK:
- IGRF-14: current International Geomagnetic Reference Field generation (2024 release) — appropriate for SAA/drift studies ✓
- NOAA WMM2025: December-2024 World Magnetic Model release — already verified in WIN-036.c3 ✓
- INTERMAGNET: real-time magnetometer observatory network — domain-appropriate ✓
- All three are publicly available at the dome monitor commit time, satisfying the post-hoc-test wrapper's claim. Cleaner than the WIN-002 companion's Sondrestrom entry.

ADVOCATE-MODE: the strongest defender line is actually self-defeating — granting that IGRF-14/WMM2025/INTERMAGNET are the source list amounts to granting that the dome consumes the same standard-model products it claims to predict. That IS the verdict ('Std Model Explains').

VERDICT MISMATCH: Verdict 'Std Model Explains' continues to rest on the dome having no independent mechanism to derive drift rate. Patch doesn't touch the mechanism critique. Verdict unaffected.

NO BATCH-CONTAMINATION CHECK: this WIN's domain (SAA westward magnetic drift) is unrelated to WIN-002's (Schumann ELF cavity). The shared structural property is only the EXP-289 templated wrapper sentence.

citations_verified: data/wins.json WIN-060 detail_verdict_text — new geomagnetic-source parenthetical present
data/wins.json WIN-060 detail_verdict_text — wrapper sentence intact
citations_unchecked: IGRF-14 release date and SAA cell drift coefficients — not re-verified this cycle but IGRF-14 is the current model generation
WMM2025 December 2024 release date — verified in WIN-036.c3 spot-check, treated as already-verified for this cycle
INTERMAGNET network identity — well-known, not re-verified

advocate_mode.best_defense: A dome defender's strongest line on the patch itself: 'IGRF-14, WMM2025, and INTERMAGNET are all standard model products derived from the same underlying observatory data the dome is comparing against. By specifying these as the source list, the review is implicitly granting that the dome\'s drift comparison rests on the same ground truth as standard MHD — which doesn\'t look like a defeat from where I\'m sitting.'
advocate_mode.rebuttal: The point of the parenthetical is to identify what data was publicly available at the time the dome monitor commit was written. IGRF-14, WMM2025, and INTERMAGNET were all public at that time — that\'s the post-hoc-test claim. Sharing the data with standard MHD is exactly what the review argues: the dome extrapolates the same drift data as NOAA/ESA, which is why the verdict is 'Std Model Explains' (data/wins.json WIN-060 finding). The defender\'s rebuttal accidentally restates the verdict.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: None introduced by the patch. EXP-289 only touches detail_verdict_text parentheticals; cross-WIN tag relations and verdict relations are unaffected.

summary_for_decider: EXP-289 substring replace on WIN-060 detail_verdict_text is bit-correct and preserves the wrapper sentence. New parenthetical (IGRF-14 / WMM2025 / INTERMAGNET) is unambiguously domain-appropriate — cleaner than the WIN-002 companion. Verdict 'Std Model Explains' unaffected. recommended_action: no_change. The 'no monitor.py entry exists for this value at all' framing in detail_verdict_text remains intact and is the rhetorical anchor of the WIN-060 post-hoc-test argument; preserve in any future edits.
